links.summary: JPMorgan and Goldman Sachs are the two dominant U.S. investment banks, competing fiercely for M&A league table supremacy. In early 2026, JPMorgan's banking leadership told staff to work harder to close the gap with Goldman, which topped the global M&A league tables for 2025. Goldman's CEO David Solomon earned $47M vs. Dimon's $43M, underscoring the rivalry at the top.

Morgan Stanley led major U.S. banks in equities revenue growth for full-year 2025 at 27.8%, but JPMorgan overtook them in Q4 with a 39.9% equities surge versus Morgan Stanley's 10.3%. Morgan Stanley posted strong investment banking results across M&A (+21%), ECM (+23%), and DCM (+26%), though its FICC trading unit underperformed peers with Q4 revenues down 8.7% year-over-year.

Both JPMorgan and Bank of America disclosed in late 2025 SEC filings that they are under federal review for alleged 'debanking' of conservative customers, following a Trump executive order targeting the practice.

JPMorgan and Citigroup are intense rivals in investment banking, particularly in debt capital markets where Citi gained ground in 2025 partly by hiring away JPMorgan bankers. Citi's Q4 2025 M&A revenues surged 84% year-over-year while JPMorgan's overall IB fees fell 5%, and Citi poached key talent like Kaustubh Kulkarni to lead its Indian operations. Both are classified as U.S. systemically important financial institutions subject to annual Fed stress tests.

Goldman Sachs and Morgan Stanley CEOs jointly warned of a 10-20% market correction at a Hong Kong summit in November 2025, triggering a global selloff. Despite this, both banks posted record or near-record 2025 results — Goldman's M&A advisory revenue rose 41% in Q4 while Morgan Stanley hit all-time highs in full-year revenue and equity trading fees.

Goldman Sachs and Bank of America compete in investment banking and equities, ranking among the top three in both categories globally. Both are deploying generative AI tools across their banking operations — Goldman rolled out an AI assistant to thousands of bankers, while Bank of America has 15 commercially live gen-AI use cases. In equity underwriting, Goldman Sachs held the top global ranking while Bank of America was a close contender throughout 2025.

Goldman Sachs and Citigroup are top-four investment banks globally and competed aggressively for M&A market share in 2025 — Goldman led the league tables while Citi's M&A revenues grew 41% in the first nine months. Both banks also jointly exited the Net-Zero Banking Alliance in late 2024/early 2025 amid political pressure, and a Bloomberg report noted both are leading Wall Street's push back into private credit, challenging private equity firms on their home turf.

Morgan Stanley and Bank of America are rivals in wealth management and investment banking. Morgan Stanley's wealth arm manages roughly $9.3T in client assets with a target of $10T by mid-2026, while Bank of America's Global Wealth unit crossed $4.6T in client balances with $84B in net flows. Both exited the Net-Zero Banking Alliance in early 2025 and are pursuing aggressive branch and advisory network expansion strategies domestically.

Morgan Stanley and Citigroup compete in investment banking and are increasingly rivals in digital assets, with both simultaneously announcing crypto custody and trading infrastructure in February 2026. There is also a notable talent pipeline between the two firms: Morgan Stanley poached senior tech banker Niall Cannon from Citi in early 2026, while Citi hired multiple Morgan Stanley wealth-tech executives including its former head of analytics, AI and data.

Bank of America and Citigroup are direct competitors as two of the Big Four U.S. banks, both subject to annual Fed stress tests and competing in consumer banking, global payments, and institutional services. Citigroup is executing a multi-year transformation to streamline operations and exit consumer banking in 14 international markets, while Bank of America is aggressively expanding its domestic branch network with 150+ new financial centers planned by 2027. Citi presented at Bank of America's own Securities Financial Services Conference in February 2026.
